title: Hans Zellner
text: Home * People * Hans Zellner Hans Zellner , a German programmer involved in the early development and compression of endgame tablebases , making three, four and even five piece endgames such as KRK (July 1985), KQK (September 1985), KPK (November 1985), KBBK (July 1986), KBNK , KQKR, and KBBKN available for home-computers , the three piece endgames and KBBK already on a Commodore 64 [1] [2] . He developed a program which perfectly played KQKR to run on an Atari ST [3] , a five-piece endgame, KBBKN had to be stored on 59 floppy disks [4] . Selected Publications [ edit ] [5] Hans Zellner ( 1986 ). Compressing Databases Down to Micro Size . ICCA Journal, Vol. 9, No. 4 Hans Zellner ( 1987 ). KBBK Squeezed into a Micro . ICCA Journal, Vol. 10, No. 1 Hans Zellner , Jaap van den Herik , Bob Herschberg ( 1987 ). Corrections and Substantiations to KBNK . ICCA Journal, Vol. 10, No. 3 Hans Zellner ( 1989 ). The KPK Database Revisited . ICCA Journal, Vol. 12, No. 2 Hans Zellner ( 1989 ). KBBKN on a Micro . ICCA Journal, Vol. 12, No. 3 References [ edit ] ↑ Jaap van den Herik , Bob Herschberg ( 1986 ). A note on KBBK . ICCA Journal, Vol. 9, No. 4 ↑ Hans Zellner ( 1986 ). Compressing Databases Down to Micro Size .
